# Transcrição: 04-Upsells Using OLAF Method

- **Fonte:** 04-Upsells Using OLAF Method
- **Duração:** 43:14

## [00:00 - 03:01]

So, this talk is how to get 30 to 50% conversion rates on your upsells using what I call the Olaf method. So, the Olaf method is something I've really devised. Like I said, over the last, it's probably four or five years, testing tons of stuff on upsells on my own offers, for clients offers, stuff like that. So, I'm going to break all this down for you before we get started. A couple things that I want you to keep in mind for this presentation. So, one of the big things, almost every direct response company, whether you're doing $10 million, $50 million, $300 million, is leaving money on the table with upsells. This has really been my observation after going through, I don't know, probably 500 to 1,000 funnels over the last couple years. Upsells are without a doubt the least optimized part of the funnel. And like I said, this really, really hits home with companies that are already doing a lot of money. So, whether you're kind of newer, whether you're advanced, upsells are probably one of the biggest spots that you're leaving money on the table. So, one thing I mentioned before earlier in the day is that you used to be able to, a couple of years ago, get away with mediocre upsells. Nowadays, that's not really possible. You can't have mediocre or just good upsells. You have to have great upsells. And the reality is, especially if you're going to scale an offer on cold traffic, that upsell has to be great. And the reason why is because it really just boosts your average order value so much. And the higher your average order value, the more traffic you can buy, the different kind of traffic sources you can get. And that's really how it offers scales. So we're going to look at a bunch of different things here. And keep in mind that when I'm talking about great upsells, my general idea for that is an upsell that converts around 30% to 50%. Anybody have any questions before we get going? I'm checking out the chat to see if there's anything in there. Cool. Don't see anything. One big thing I want to talk about here for copywriters. and we're gonna break down how they work. So the first one is the offer. This is without a doubt the most important part of the upsell. When I say the offer, I mean what you're actually selling. There's nothing that works better than done for you because that's really what people want. They want the result and they don't want to have to do any of the work. ## [27:13 - 30:13]

So your video is like two minutes long or the copy on the page is like this long. This is a huge, huge, huge mistake on upsells. You have to go through the whole selling process, just like you would on a regular sales page. So the best upsells that I have seen are almost always 15 to 20 minutes long. I've even seen something like 25 minutes long, longer is better on upsells. We always talk about longer copy working on the front end and it almost always beats shorter copy. This translates to upsells as well. This took me a long time to learn because I was one of those people with the T-Rex arms trying to do the two minute video and the little short copy. Because I didn't think someone would read it or someone would watch it, especially if they just watched the 40 minute video sales letter. But that's not the case at all. From everything I've tested, the best upsells are always longer. So whether that's video or long copy, you want to make sure your copy is long. I'll give you a little example of this. So a client a few years ago, I ran a test for them. They had video on their page. Their original video was two minutes and 45 seconds. We tested one that was seven minutes and I wanted to make one that was like 15 minutes long. They didn't want one that was 15 minutes long because they thought it was too long. I went in and did a test of all three and these are the conversion rates of all three of them. This offer obviously needed a lot more work to really get it dialed in. But this is just a really clear example of the longer the video, the longer the copy, the better it's going to convert. So overall, it went from 12% to like 17%, which was like a 39% boost in conversions, simply by making the upsell longer. So a big takeaway for you on this one, on the length. So we're on the L part of Olof, which is the length. Whether your upsell is video, whether it's text, you want longer copy. Like I said, usually 15, 20, 25 minutes. All the offers that I shared earlier, those ones that are like crushing, almost all of them are between 15 and 25 minutes long. So you might be thinking kind of what should I hit on in my upsell copy if I'm going to write 15 minutes of copy, if I'm going to write 20 minutes of copy, 25, whatever. Here's a kind of quick overview of what my process looks like. So it's an opening where I congratulate them on the purchase. This is a huge important part. We're going to go over this more when we get to the next part. Future pacing. So I'm going to talk about all the benefits that are coming their way. And I talk about this for a while. This is like three to five minutes of the opening. Present a new problem to them. So fixing your credit is hard. You might not want to do it. That's a new problem to them. Then I'm going to bring up issues that might happen if they don't actually fix the new problem. So you just bought this product. And if you don't do anything, here's what's going to happen. Here's how you're going to be broken in, not being able to get credit, stuff like that. Hit on the benefits of what you're about to present to them.

## [30:13 - 33:15]

Then you go into present the upsell. Then you do your normal price justification. So this is normally 97 bucks, but today it's $47. And here's why. And then the closing. You really want to encompass a lot in the close. So you want to like final pitch, remind them of what they're getting, restate the benefits, talk about how it really is a one-time offer. If they leave this page, you're never going to be able to get it again. Special price, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah. So that's kind of just a quick overview of what the process looks like in terms of the length. Does that make sense for you guys? Let me give me something in the chat so I know what's going on. Awesome. So let's go to the next one. So we covered the O, we covered the L. The next O is the opening. Now, this is really a new addition to my upsell formula. And this is all based on what J.D. Bult taught at the Vegas event. If any of you guys watched the video that's in the members area that Jay talked about at that event, it's honestly one of the best talks I've ever seen at an event. It completely blew my mind. I've since implemented a lot of what Jay talked about on funnels for two of my clients. And I've seen people in the copy accelerator group implemented as well, and it just crushes over and over and over again. So here's kind of the big mistake people make with their openings is really just going right into pitching the customer on another product. The reason why it's such a huge mistake is you got to think about it from the customer's point of view. Most people buy something online and they expect to go to a confirmation page that shows them, here's your order, blah, blah, blah. If they all of a sudden come onto a page and you're just pitching them on another product, they start to feel like, am I on the wrong page? Did something break? Is something going wrong? They get all kinds of anxiety. They also get a bunch of anxiety about the purchase they just made because they're wondering, did I just get scammed? There's all this shit going through their head that if you don't alleviate it, you're not going to make that next sale. So Jay really hit on this. This is something I had actually always thought about before, but Jay really just kind of drilled that down at the Vegas event.
